perturbation.original_text: Each disclosing Party (each a "Discloser") would not disclose its Confidential Information without the Recipient's agreement to maintain the Confidential Information in confidence and to use such Confidential Information solely as permitted under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each disclosing Party (each a "Discloser") may disclose its Confidential Information at its sole discretion. The Recipient is not obligated to maintain the Confidential Information in confidence unless explicitly agreed upon in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of the NDA. The original text establishes a clear expectation of confidentiality. The changed text allows the Discloser to reveal information freely and removes the Recipient's obligation to keep it secret unless otherwise agreed. This creates an in-text contradiction as the entire agreement is based on an initial assumption of confidentiality, making the subsequent clauses on non-use and non-disclosure nonsensical.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Use and Non-Disclosure. Recipients will not disclose, distribute, copy or use any Confidential Information or the fact that a Transaction is being considered or is under a production contract without the prior written consent of the other Party and then only to the extent specified in such consent. Confidential Information may be used only to the extent reasonably required for the purposes of evaluating, producing or carrying out the Transaction; provided however, that Recipients may only disclose the Confidential Information to those of its contractors, vendors, directors, officers, advisors, agents and employees ("Representatives") who need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating, performing or carrying out or producing the Transaction and who shall be bound by a written agreement or other legally enforceable written obligations to preserve the confidentiality of the Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Discloser to reveal confidential information freely and removes the Recipient's obligation to keep it secret unless otherwise agreed in writing. This directly contradicts the 'Non-Use and Non-Disclosure' clause, which states that Recipients will not disclose or use any Confidential Information without prior written consent. The change fundamentally undermines the confidentiality expectations established in the contradicted text, leading to a strong in-text contradiction and making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information. However, the recipient is not responsible for inadvertent disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, the recipient is not responsible for inadvertent disclosures' creates an in-text contradiction within Section 3. The first part of the sentence mandates 'reasonable measures' to protect Confidential Information, implying a responsibility to prevent disclosures. The added clause negates this by excusing 'inadvertent disclosures,' which could be interpreted broadly to undermine the entire section's intent and raises questions about what constitutes 'reasonable measures' if inadvertent disclosures are not considered a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidentiality. Recipient agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information. However, the recipient is not responsible for inadvertent disclosures. Without limiting the foregoing, Recipient shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than reasonable measures. Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original. Recipient shall immediately notify Discloser upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information. If the Recipient receives a request to disclose all or any part of the Confidential Information under the terms of a valid subpoena or order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental body, Recipient agrees to (i) immediately notify the Discloser of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, so that the Discloser may seek an appropriate protective order or other reliable written assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to Confidential Information, and (ii) only disclose that portion of the Confidential Information which counsel advises is legally required to be disclosed. At all times, the Discloser shall retain sole ownership of its Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, the recipient is not responsible for inadvertent disclosures.' to the original text. This directly contradicts the original requirement for 'reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure'. By excusing 'inadvertent disclosures,' it undermines the responsibility implied by 'reasonable measures' creating a direct conflict and uncertainty as to the extent of the recipient's obligations. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction where different sections (in this case, different parts of the same section) provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will promptly return to the Discloser or at Discloser’s instructions destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information and all physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof, with a letter confirming that the Confidential Information has not been reproduced or copied or that all copies have been returned and that none of the Confidential Information was disclosed to anyone except in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will promptly return to the Discloser or at Discloser’s instructions destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information and all physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof, with a letter confirming that the Confidential Information has not been reproduced or copied or that all copies have been returned and that none of the Confidential Information was disclosed to anyone except in accordance with this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain indefinitely any Confidential Information necessary for compliance with internal record-keeping policies.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain indefinitely any Confidential Information necessary for compliance with internal record-keeping policies' creates an in-text contradiction with the first part of Section 4. The initial clause demands prompt return or destruction of Confidential Information upon request, ensuring no unauthorized copies remain. The added clause allows indefinite retention for internal record-keeping, directly contradicting the obligation to return or destroy all copies and undermines the intended security and control over the Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials. At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will promptly return to the Discloser or at Discloser’s instructions destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information and all physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof, with a letter confirming that the Confidential Information has not been reproduced or copied or that all copies have been returned and that none of the Confidential Information was disclosed to anyone except in accordance with this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient may retain indefinitely any Confidential Information necessary for compliance with internal record-keeping policies. The Recipient and its Representatives shall not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions, in whole or in part, of such written or electronic material, except as may be needed to comply with applicable law and, provided that Recipient may retain one copy of Confidential Information in its confidential files for use only in the event a dispute arises between the Parties hereunder and only in connection with that dispute.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that the Recipient may retain indefinitely any Confidential Information necessary for compliance with internal record-keeping policies, while the original text and contradicted text state that the Recipient will promptly return or destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information. The addition directly contradicts the obligation to return or destroy all copies and undermines the intended security and control over the Discloser's Confidential Information. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient's obligations under this Agreement with respect to disclosures of Confidential Information made hereunder shall expire five (5) years from the date this Agreement is executed by the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient's obligations under this Agreement with respect to disclosures of Confidential Information made hereunder shall expire five (5) years from the date this Agreement is executed by the Parties, unless the Discloser notifies the Recipient in writing that the obligations shall continue indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless the Discloser notifies the Recipient in writing that the obligations shall continue indefinitely' creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original clause establishes a definitive five-year expiration of confidentiality obligations. The added clause allows the Discloser to unilaterally extend these obligations indefinitely. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the actual term of the agreement and undermines the Recipient's expectation of a limited timeframe for confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. Recipient's obligations under this Agreement with respect to disclosures of Confidential Information made hereunder shall expire five (5) years from the date this Agreement is executed by the Parties, unless the Discloser notifies the Recipient in writing that the obligations shall continue indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing the Discloser to unilaterally extend the confidentiality obligations indefinitely, contradicting the original fixed five-year expiration. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted text repeats this changed text, leading to uncertainty about the actual term of the agreement as the obligations can continue indefinitely, against the initially defined term. This follows the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections provide conflicting obligations.
